name: Windmill Workflow Automation Platform
what_it_does: Open-source developer platform for building and running scripts, workflows (flows), and internal apps across Python, TypeScript, Go, Bash, and SQL with a visual flow editor and REST API for programmatic control.
use_cases: Running heterogeneous agent scripts across multiple languages in a unified orchestration platform
Building internal automation workflows that combine Python data processing, TypeScript API calls, and SQL queries
Replacing n8n or Zapier for code-first automation with full version control and testing
Scheduling and monitoring batch jobs with dependency management and secret injection
Programmatically triggering flows and scripts from an agent via REST API
not_for: Consumer-facing workflow products (Windmill is developer/internal-tools focused)
Ultra-high-throughput event streaming or message queue workloads
Teams that need guaranteed enterprise SLAs without hosting their own infrastructure
Non-technical users who need no-code automation (n8n or Zapier are better fits)
best_when: A team needs to orchestrate scripts and workflows across multiple languages with a clean REST API for agent control, code versioning, and the option to self-host for full data control.
avoid_when: You need a fully managed, zero-ops platform; your workflows are exclusively TypeScript and Inngest/Trigger.dev would suffice; or you need consumer-facing automation features.

auth.notes: API tokens are scoped to a workspace and can have expiration dates. OAuth2 integration for SSO (GitHub, GitLab, Google, SAML) on enterprise tier. Token-level permissions map to workspace roles. Service accounts supported.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted is free (AGPL-3.0). Windmill Cloud: 1,000 executions/month free on the Community plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Team plan $10/seat/month (includes 10,000 executions/seat); Enterprise self-hosted from $500/month for dedicated support and EE features
pricing.notes: Self-hosting (Docker Compose or Kubernetes) is fully featured and free. Cloud is convenient for small teams. Enterprise tier unlocks audit logs, SSO, and dedicated workers. Execution = one script or flow step run.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: limited only by worker capacity. Cloud: concurrent job limits per plan tier; free tier allows 2 concurrent workers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors with clear status codes. Script and flow execution errors are captured in job logs accessible via API. Error propagation between flow steps is configurable (stop-on-error vs continue).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Job triggers return a job UUID for status polling. No built-in idempotency keys on the trigger endpoint — duplicate triggers create duplicate jobs. Idempotency must be implemented in the script logic itself.
known_agent_gotchas: AGPL-3.0 license on the server requires careful legal review for commercial self-hosted deployments
Flow steps share context via result passing, not a shared state store — complex state management requires explicit passing or external storage
The visual flow editor and REST API both work well, but some advanced flow features (error handlers, approval steps) are only configurable via the UI, not purely via API
Windmill Cloud free tier (1,000 executions/month) is very low for agent workloads — exhausted quickly in development
Cold start latency on serverless workers can add several hundred milliseconds for infrequently triggered scripts
OpenAPI spec is auto-generated and comprehensive but can lag behind latest API features by a release

security_score_components.security_notes: Built-in secrets management (variables and resources) with encryption at rest. TLS enforced on cloud endpoints. Workspace-scoped tokens with role-based access. Self-hosted security depends on operator configuration. AGPL license requires legal review for commercial deployments.
